title: graph2otel: Entra ID compliance overview
description: Entra ID directory, licensing, application, policy, role, secure-score, MFA and risk posture, plus the log twins that name the user, app or sign-in behind each number. Generated by grafana/build_dashboard.py from spec/signal-catalog.json — edit grafana/boards/entra_compliance.py, not this JSON.

list.name: datasource
list.label: Prometheus datasource
list.description: Where graph2otel's OTLP metrics land after normalization.

list.name: loki_datasource
list.label: Loki datasource
list.description: Required by the log panels. Leave unset and they say so rather than looking broken.

list.name: tenant
list.label: Tenant
list.description: tenant_id is on every signal (#143). A single-tenant deployment with no tenant id configured stamps no label; select All.

panels.title: Read this before writing your own query

options.content:
**Metrics answer "how many"; logs answer "which one".** graph2otel puts bounded,
tenant-shaped aggregates on metrics and per-entity detail on the log twin (#112).
If a panel here cannot tell you *which* device or user, the Logs row at the bottom
can.

**Metric names are the OTLP→Prometheus normalized form** — dots become underscores
and unit/type suffixes are appended (`_total` on counters, `_seconds`, `_ratio`,
`_percent`). Every query on this dashboard is generated from
`spec/signal-catalog.json`, which is itself generated from what the collectors
actually emit, so a panel cannot name a metric that does not exist.

**LogQL: attributes are structured metadata, not stream labels.** Only
`service_name` is a stream label. `{event_name="entra.signin"}` matches **zero rows,
silently** — it is not an error. Always:

```logql
{service_name="graph2otel"} | event_name=`entra.signin` | status_error_code!=`0`
```

**Empty is often correct.** Several collectors are opt-in (blob ingest, beta Graph
surfaces, high-volume feeds) and several are empty on a healthy tenant. A blank panel
is not evidence of a broken pipeline.

targets.legendFormat: Directory sync last sync age seconds

panels.title: Auth methods policy legacy enabled total
Secure score control profiles by category
Secure score control profiles by status
MFA registration admin MFA capable total
Risk and sign-ins (Entra ID P2 for the risk signals)
Logs — which one, not how many (#162)
Failed sign-ins — which user, which IP, which error
panels.description:
The metric says how many sign-ins failed. This says whose. Error 53004 is the Tor/anonymous-IP case #129 used to prove risk detection fires.

Needs a **Loki** datasource (selected above). Log attributes are Loki *structured metadata*, not stream labels: filter with `| event_name=…` after `{service_name="graph2otel"}`. A stream selector on an attribute matches zero rows silently (#90).

defaults.noValue: No rows. Either no Loki datasource is selected above, or nothing matched. Log attributes are structured metadata: filter with | attr=… after {service_name="graph2otel"}, never {attr="…"}.

panels.title: Failed sign-in rate by error code
panels.description: Needs a **Loki** datasource (selected above). Log attributes are Loki *structured metadata*, not stream labels: filter with `| event_name=…` after `{service_name="graph2otel"}`. A stream selector on an attribute matches zero rows silently (#90).

defaults.noValue: No rows. Either no Loki datasource is selected above, or nothing matched. Log attributes are structured metadata: filter with | attr=… after {service_name="graph2otel"}, never {attr="…"}.

panels.title: Top failing sign-in sources (country, client app)
panels.description: Needs a **Loki** datasource (selected above). Log attributes are Loki *structured metadata*, not stream labels: filter with `| event_name=…` after `{service_name="graph2otel"}`. A stream selector on an attribute matches zero rows silently (#90).

defaults.noValue: No rows. Either no Loki datasource is selected above, or nothing matched. Log attributes are structured metadata: filter with | attr=… after {service_name="graph2otel"}, never {attr="…"}.

targets.expr: topk(20, sum by (location_country_or_region, client_app_used) (count_over_time({service_name="graph2otel"} | event_name=`entra.signin` | tenant_id=~"$tenant" | status_error_code!=`0` [$__auto])))

panels.title: Risk detections — MITRE ATT&CK technique, IP, user
panels.description:
mitre_techniques is on every risk detection (#153). Add `| mitre_techniques=~"T1090.*"` to this panel's query to filter to proxy/tunnelling techniques.

Needs a **Loki** datasource (selected above). Log attributes are Loki *structured metadata*, not stream labels: filter with `| event_name=…` after `{service_name="graph2otel"}`. A stream selector on an attribute matches zero rows silently (#90).

defaults.noValue: No rows. Either no Loki datasource is selected above, or nothing matched. Log attributes are structured metadata: filter with | attr=… after {service_name="graph2otel"}, never {attr="…"}.

panels.title: Risk detections by event type and level
panels.description: Needs a **Loki** datasource (selected above). Log attributes are Loki *structured metadata*, not stream labels: filter with `| event_name=…` after `{service_name="graph2otel"}`. A stream selector on an attribute matches zero rows silently (#90).

defaults.noValue: No rows. Either no Loki datasource is selected above, or nothing matched. Log attributes are structured metadata: filter with | attr=… after {service_name="graph2otel"}, never {attr="…"}.

targets.expr: topk(20, sum by (risk_event_type, risk_level) (count_over_time({service_name="graph2otel"} | event_name=`entra.risk_detection` | tenant_id=~"$tenant" [$__auto])))

panels.title: Top directory audit activities
panels.description: Needs a **Loki** datasource (selected above). Log attributes are Loki *structured metadata*, not stream labels: filter with `| event_name=…` after `{service_name="graph2otel"}`. A stream selector on an attribute matches zero rows silently (#90).

defaults.noValue: No rows. Either no Loki datasource is selected above, or nothing matched. Log attributes are structured metadata: filter with | attr=… after {service_name="graph2otel"}, never {attr="…"}.

targets.expr: topk(20, sum by (activity_display_name, result) (count_over_time({service_name="graph2otel"} | event_name=`entra.directory_audit` | tenant_id=~"$tenant" [$__auto])))
